title: tutcheria tawauensis
intro: Tutcheria tawauensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is also known as Tawau fruit or Tawau tree, and is native to Borneo. The plant has been reported to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids. The fruit and leaves of Tutcheria tawauensis are used in traditional medicine, often prepared as decoctions or infusions to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
